   501→
   502→  const pendingMessages = messages.filter((m) => m.status === 'pending')
   503→  const runningAgents = agents.filter((a) => a.status === 'running')
   504→
   505→  function renderAgentNode(agent: Agent, allAgents: Agent[], depth: number): React.ReactNode {
   506→    const children = allAgents.filter((a) => a.reports_to === agent.id)
   507→    return (
   508→      <div key={agent.id} className="relative pl-6 pt-3">
   509→        {/* Connector line */}
   510→        <div className="absolute left-0 top-6 h-px w-6 bg-[#d8e6dc]" />
   511→
   512→        <div className={`rounded-xl border p-3 ${agent.status === 'running' ? 'border-[#7fd4a0] bg-[#f0fdf4]' : 'border-[#d8e6dc] bg-white'}`}>
   513→          <div className="flex items-center justify-between gap-2">
   514→            <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
   515→              <span className={`h-2.5 w-2.5 rounded-full ${agent.status === 'running' ? 'bg-[#35bf68] animate-dot' : 'bg-[#abc8b4]'}`} />
   516→              <span className="text-sm font-bold text-[#26372d]">{agent.title}</span>
   517→              {agent.rank && (
   518→                <span className={`rounded-full px-2 py-0.5 text-[10px] font-semibold ${RANK_THEME[agent.rank] ?? 'bg-[#e7f2ea] text-[#446658]'}`}>
   519→                  {agent.rank}
   520→                </span>
   521→              )}
   522→            </div>
   523→            <button onClick={() => fireAgent(agent.id)} className="text-[11px] font-semibold text-[#9d5d52] hover:text-[#b44136]">
   524→              fire
   525→            </button>
   526→          </div>
   527→          <p className="mt-1 font-mono text-[10.5px] text-[#6f887b]">{agent.id}</p>
   528→          <p className="mt-0.5 text-xs leading-relaxed text-[#647d71]">{agent.description}</p>
   529→          {agent.status === 'running' && (
   530→            <button
   531→              onClick={() => fetch(`/api/run/${agent.id}`, { method: 'DELETE' })}
   532→              className="btn-base btn-danger mt-2 py-1 text-xs"
   533→            >
   534→              Kill session
   535→            </button>
   536→          )}
   537→        </div>
   538→
   539→        {children.length > 0 && (
   540→          <div className="ml-4 border-l-2 border-[#d8e6dc]">
   541→            {children.map((child) => renderAgentNode(child, allAgents, depth + 1))}
   542→          </div>
   543→        )}
   544→      </div>
   545→    )
   546→  }
   547→
   548→  function renderDeptTeamTree(team: Team) {
   549→    // Get all agents in this team
   550→    const headAgent = agents.find((a) => a.id === team.head)
   551→    const memberAgents = agents.filter((a) => team.members.includes(a.id))
   552→    const allTeamAgents = headAgent ? [headAgent, ...memberAgents] : memberAgents
   553→    const hasRunning = allTeamAgents.some((a) => a.status === 'running')
   554→    const total = allTeamAgents.length
   555→
   556→    return (
   557→      <div key={team.id} className="relative pl-6 pt-3">
   558→        <div className="absolute left-0 top-6 h-px w-6 bg-[#d8e6dc]" />
   559→
   560→        {/* Department node — clickable to expand */}
   561→        <button
   562→          onClick={() => setExpandedDept(expandedDept === team.id ? null : team.id)}
   563→          className={`w-full rounded-xl border p-3 text-left transition-colors ${
   564→            expandedDept === team.id
   565→              ? 'border-[#a8c8f0] bg-[#f0f6ff]'
   566→              : 'border-[#d8e6dc] bg-white hover:bg-[#f8fdf9]'
   567→          }`}
   568→        >
   569→          <div className="flex items-center justify-between gap-2">
   570→            <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
   571→              {hasRunning && (
   572→                <span className="h-2.5 w-2.5 rounded-full bg-[#35bf68] animate-dot" />
   573→              )}
   574→              <span className={`rounded-full px-2 py-0.5 text-[11px] font-semibold ${DEPT_THEME[team.name] ?? 'bg-[#e7f2ea] text-[#446658]'}`}>
   575→                {team.name}
   576→              </span>
   577→              <span className="rounded-full bg-white/80 px-2 py-0.5 text-[11px] font-semibold text-[#55695f]">
   578→                {total}
   579→              </span>
   580→            </div>
   581→            <span className="text-xs text-[#8a9e91]">
   582→              {expandedDept === team.id ? '▼' : '▶'}
   583→            </span>
   584→          </div>
   585→          {headAgent && (
   586→            <p className="mt-1 text-xs text-[#647d71]">
   587→              Head: {headAgent.title}
   588→            </p>
   589→          )}
   590→        </button>
   591→
   592→        {/* Expanded internal tree */}
   593→        {expandedDept === team.id && (
   594→          <div className="ml-4 border-l-2 border-[#c7d8f0]">
   595→            {headAgent && renderAgentNode(headAgent, allTeamAgents, 0)}
   596→            {/* Members without a reports_to chain within the team (orphans within dept) */}
   597→            {memberAgents
   598→              .filter((a) => a.reports_to !== team.head && !allTeamAgents.some((t) => t.id === a.reports_to))
   599→              .map((agent) => renderAgentNode(agent, allTeamAgents, 0))}
   600→          </div>
   601→        )}
   602→      </div>
   603→    )
   604→  }
   605→
   606→  return (
   607→    <div className="app-shell">
   608→      <div className="shell-card">
   609→        <header className="border-b border-[var(--line-soft)] px-4 pb-3 pt-4 lg:px-5">
   610→          <div className="flex flex-col gap-4 lg:flex-row lg:items-start lg:justify-between">
   611→            <div className="max-w-xl">
   612→              <h1 className="font-display text-[1.9rem] leading-[1] text-[#1e3127]">ClawCorp Control</h1>
   613→              <p className="mt-1 text-sm text-[var(--text-muted)]">Operate missions across the task board with minimum friction.</p>
   614→            </div>
   615→
   616→            <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-2 lg:justify-end">
   617→              <span className="metric-pill">{missions.length} missions</span>
   618→              <span className="metric-pill warm">{pendingMessages.length} pending inbox</span>
   619→              <span className="metric-pill sky">{agents.length} active agents</span>
   620→              <span className="metric-pill">
   621→                {runningAgents.length} running
   622→                {runningAgents.length > 0 && (
   623→                  <span className="ml-1 inline-block h-1.5 w-1.5 rounded-full bg-[#2fb060] align-middle animate-dot" />
   624→                )}
   625→              </span>
   626→            </div>
   627→          </div>
   628→
   629→          <div className="mt-4 flex flex-wrap items-center gap-2">
   630→            {(['tasks', 'inbox', 'agents'] as const).map((t) => (
   631→              <button key={t} onClick={() => setTab(t)} className={`tab-chip ${tab === t ? 'active' : ''}`}>
   632→                {t}
   633→                {t === 'inbox' && pendingMessages.length > 0 ? ` (${pendingMessages.length})` : ''}
   634→                {t === 'tasks' && tasks.length > 0 ? ` (${tasks.length})` : ''}
   635→              </button>
   636→            ))}
   637→
   638→            {hiringInProgress && (
   639→              <span className="ml-auto flex items-center gap-1.5 rounded-full bg-[#fff6e9] px-3 py-1 text-xs font-semibold text-[#8d5b1d]">
   640→                <span className="inline-block h-1.5 w-1.5 rounded-full bg-[#d4aa4f] animate-dot" />
   641→                Interviewing candidates...
   642→              </span>
   643→            )}
   644→          </div>
   645→        </header>
   646→
   647→        <div className="flex min-h-0 flex-1 flex-col xl:flex-row">
   648→          <main className="min-h-0 flex-1 overflow-y-auto p-2 lg:p-3">
   649→            {tab === 'inbox' && (
   650→              <section className="space-y-3 pt-1">
   651→                <div className="section-label">Organization Inbox</div>
   652→
   653→                {messages.length === 0 && (
   654→                  <div className="panel p-5 text-sm text-[var(--text-muted)]">No messages yet. Escalations and reports will appear here.</div>
   655→                )}
   656→
   657→                <div className="space-y-3">
   658→                  {messages.map((msg, idx) => (
   659→                    <article
   660→                      key={msg.id}
   661→                      className={`panel animate-rise p-4 ${MESSAGE_THEME[msg.type]}`}
   662→                      style={{ animationDelay: `${idx * 0.03}s` }}
   663→                    >
   664→                      <div className="flex flex-wrap items-center justify-between gap-2">
   665→                        <div className="flex flex-wrap items-center gap-2 text-xs">
   666→                          <span className="font-semibold text-[#305e42]">{msg.from}</span>
   667→                          <span className="text-[#70897c]">to {msg.to}</span>
   668→                          <span
   669→                            className={`rounded-full px-2 py-0.5 text-[11px] font-semibold ${
   670→                              msg.type === 'report' ? 'bg-[#d8f0dd] text-[#2f7044]' : 'bg-[#f7e3c8] text-[#8d5b1d]'
   671→                            }`}
   672→                          >
   673→                            {msg.type}
   674→                          </span>
   675→                          <span className="font-mono text-[11px] text-[#6c8578]">{msg.missionId}</span>
   676→                        </div>
   677→
   678→                        <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
   679→                          <span className="font-mono text-[10.5px] text-[#759083]">{formatMessageTime(msg.created_at)}</span>
   680→                          <span
   681→                            className={`rounded-full px-2 py-0.5 text-xs font-semibold ${
   682→                              msg.status === 'pending' ? 'bg-[#fff2dd] text-[#97631f]' : 'bg-[#e4f5e9] text-[#3b6f49]'
   683→                            }`}
   684→                          >
   685→                            {msg.status}
   686→                          </span>
   687→                        </div>
   688→                      </div>
   689→
   690→                      <p className="mt-2 text-sm text-[#2b3a31]">{msg.type === 'report' ? msg.summary : msg.question}</p>
   691→
   692→                      {msg.context && <p className="mt-1 text-xs italic text-[#678074]">{msg.context}</p>}
   693→
   694→                      {msg.artifacts && msg.artifacts.length > 0 && (
   695→                        <div className="mt-2 space-y-1 rounded-lg bg-white/70 p-2 font-mono text-[11px] text-[#6b8277]">
   696→                          {msg.artifacts.map((a, i) => (
   697→                            <div key={i}>{a}</div>
   698→                          ))}
   699→                        </div>
   700→                      )}
   701→
   702→                      {msg.status === 'answered' && msg.answer && (
   703→                        <p className="mt-2 rounded-lg bg-[#e9f7ed] px-2.5 py-2 text-sm text-[#376b47]">Answer: {msg.answer}</p>
   704→                      )}
   705→
   706→                      {msg.status === 'pending' && msg.type === 'escalate' && (
   707→                        <button onClick={() => setAnswerTarget(msg)} className="btn-base btn-primary mt-3 py-1.5 text-xs">
   708→                          Answer escalation
   709→                        </button>
   710→                      )}
   711→                    </article>
   712→                  ))}
   713→                </div>
   714→              </section>
   715→            )}
   716→
   717→            {tab === 'agents' && (
   718→              <section className="space-y-3 pt-1">
   719→                <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
   720→                  <div className="section-label">Organization ({agents.length})</div>
   721→                  <button onClick={() => setShowSmartHire(true)} className="btn-base btn-primary py-1.5 text-xs">
   722→                    Smart Hire
   723→                  </button>
   724→                </div>
   725→
   726→                {/* Org tree */}
   727→                <div className="panel animate-rise p-4">
   728→                  {/* Chairman root */}
   729→                  <div className="flex items-center gap-2 rounded-xl border border-[#d4c9a8] bg-[#fdf8ec] px-4 py-3">
   730→                    <span className="h-3 w-3 rounded-full bg-[#d4aa4f]" />
   731→                    <span className="text-base font-bold text-[#5a4b1e]">Chairman</span>
   732→                    <span className="rounded-full bg-[#f0e4be] px-2 py-0.5 text-[11px] font-semibold text-[#8a7328]">You</span>
   733→                  </div>
   734→
   735→                  {/* C-suite executives (agents that report to chairman) */}
   736→                  <div className="ml-6 border-l-2 border-[#d8e6dc]">
   737→                    {agents
   738→                      .filter((a) => a.reports_to === 'chairman')
   739→                      .map((exec) => {
   740→                        // Find departments this executive sponsors
   741→                        const sponsoredTeams = teams.filter((t) => t.executive_sponsor === exec.id)
   742→
   743→                        return (
   744→                          <div key={exec.id} className="relative pl-6 pt-3">
   745→                            <div className="absolute left-0 top-6 h-px w-6 bg-[#d8e6dc]" />
   746→
   747→                            {/* Executive node */}
   748→                            <div className={`rounded-xl border p-3 ${exec.status === 'running' ? 'border-[#7fd4a0] bg-[#f0fdf4]' : 'border-[#d4c9a8] bg-[#fefcf5]'}`}>
   749→                              <div className="flex items-center justify-between gap-2">
   750→                                <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
   751→                                  <span className={`h-2.5 w-2.5 rounded-full ${exec.status === 'running' ? 'bg-[#35bf68] animate-dot' : 'bg-[#d4aa4f]'}`} />
   752→                                  <span className="text-sm font-bold text-[#26372d]">{exec.title}</span>
   753→                                  <span className={`rounded-full px-2 py-0.5 text-[10px] font-semibold ${RANK_THEME.executive}`}>
   754→                                    executive
   755→                                  </span>
   756→                                </div>
   757→                                <button onClick={() => fireAgent(exec.id)} className="text-[11px] font-semibold text-[#9d5d52] hover:text-[#b44136]">
   758→                                  fire
   759→                                </button>
   760→                              </div>
   761→                              <p className="mt-1 font-mono text-[10.5px] text-[#6f887b]">{exec.id}</p>
   762→                              <p className="mt-0.5 text-xs leading-relaxed text-[#647d71]">{exec.description}</p>
   763→                              {exec.status === 'running' && (
   764→                                <button
   765→                                  onClick={() => fetch(`/api/run/${exec.id}`, { method: 'DELETE' })}
   766→                                  className="btn-base btn-danger mt-2 py-1 text-xs"
   767→                                >
   768→                                  Kill session
   769→                                </button>
   770→                              )}
   771→                            </div>
   772→
   773→                            {/* Departments under this executive */}
   774→                            {sponsoredTeams.length > 0 && (
   775→                              <div className="ml-4 border-l-2 border-[#d8e6dc]">
   776→                                {sponsoredTeams.map((team) => renderDeptTeamTree(team))}
   777→                              </div>
   778→                            )}
   779→                          </div>
   780→                        )
   781→                      })}
   782→                  </div>
   783→
   784→                  {/* Unassigned agents (no team, not an executive) */}
   785→                  {(() => {
   786→                    const teamAgentIds = new Set<string>()
   787→                    for (const t of teams) {
   788→                      if (t.head) teamAgentIds.add(t.head)
   789→                      for (const m of t.members) teamAgentIds.add(m)
   790→                    }
   791→                    const execs = agents.filter((a) => a.reports_to === 'chairman')
   792→                    const execIds = new Set(execs.map((e) => e.id))
   793→                    const unassigned = agents.filter((a) => !teamAgentIds.has(a.id) && !execIds.has(a.id))
   794→
   795→                    if (unassigned.length === 0) return null
   796→
   797→                    return (
   798→                      <div className="mt-4">
   799→                        <div className="section-label text-[11px]">Unassigned</div>
   800→                        <div className="ml-6 border-l-2 border-dashed border-[#e0d8c8]">
   801→                          {unassigned.map((agent) => renderAgentNode(agent, unassigned, 0))}
   802→                        </div>
   803→                      </div>
   804→                    )
   805→                  })()}
   806→                </div>
   807→              </section>
   808→            )}
   809→
   810→            {tab === 'tasks' && (
   811→              <div className="flex min-h-full flex-col">
   812→                <div className="mb-3 flex items-center gap-2">
   813→                  <input
   814→                    value={newTaskTitle}
   815→                    onChange={(e) => setNewTaskTitle(e.target.value)}
   816→                    onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter') createTask() }}
   817→                    placeholder="New task title — press Enter"
   818→                    className="input-base h-9 min-w-0 flex-1 bg-white"
   819→                  />
   820→                  <button
   821→                    onClick={createTask}
   822→                    disabled={!newTaskTitle.trim()}
   823→                    className="btn-base btn-primary h-9 px-4 py-0 text-xs"
   824→                  >
   825→                    Add
   826→                  </button>
   827→                </div>
   828→
   829→                <section className="panel animate-rise flex min-h-0 flex-1 flex-col p-2 lg:p-3">
   830→                  <div className="kanban-scroll h-full min-h-0 flex-1">
   831→                    {TASK_COLUMNS.map((col, colIndex) => {
   832→                      const colTasks = tasks.filter((t) => t.status === col)
   833→                      const colTheme = TASK_THEME[col]
   834→
   835→                      return (
   836→                        <div
   837→                          key={col}
   838→                          onDragOver={(e) => e.preventDefault()}
   839→                          onDrop={() => dropOnTaskColumn(col)}
   840→                          className={`lane-card panel flex h-full min-h-0 flex-col p-3 ${colTheme.lane} ${dragTask ? 'ring-1 ring-[#74b388]/70' : ''}`}
   841→                          style={{ animationDelay: `${colIndex * 0.03}s` }}
   842→                        >
   843→                          <div className="mb-2 flex items-center justify-between">
   844→                            <span className={`rounded-full px-2.5 py-1 text-[11px] font-extrabold uppercase tracking-[0.08em] ${colTheme.badge}`}>
   845→                              {TASK_LABELS[col]}
   846→                            </span>
   847→                            <span className="rounded-full bg-white/80 px-2 py-0.5 text-xs font-semibold text-[#55695f]">
   848→                              {colTasks.length}
   849→                            </span>
   850→                          </div>
   851→
   852→                          <div className="min-h-0 flex-1 space-y-2.5 overflow-y-auto pr-1">
   853→                            {colTasks.length === 0 && (
   854→                              <div className={`rounded-xl border border-dashed p-3 text-xs ${colTheme.empty}`}>
   855→                                Drop tasks here
   856→                              </div>
   857→                            )}
   858→
   859→                            {colTasks.map((t, taskIndex) => (
   860→                              <article
   861→                                key={t.id}
   862→                                draggable
   863→                                onDragStart={() => setDragTask(t.id)}
   864→                                onDragEnd={() => setDragTask(null)}
   865→                                className="mission-card animate-rise cursor-grab p-3 active:cursor-grabbing"
   866→                                style={{ animationDelay: `${taskIndex * 0.02}s` }}
   867→                              >
   868→                                <p className="text-sm font-bold leading-snug text-[#24342b]">{t.title}</p>
   869→                                <p className="mt-1 font-mono text-[10.5px] text-[#6c8578]">{t.id}</p>
   870→
   871→                                {t.feedback.length > 0 && (
   872→                                  <div className="mt-2 space-y-1">
   873→                                    <p className="rounded-lg bg-[#fff6e9] px-2 py-1.5 text-xs text-[#7a5c2e]">
   874→                                      {t.feedback[t.feedback.length - 1].text}
   875→                                    </p>
   876→                                    {t.feedback.length > 1 && (
   877→                                      <details className="text-[11px] text-[#8a9e91]">
   878→                                        <summary className="cursor-pointer hover:text-[#5a7266]">
   879→                                          {t.feedback.length - 1} earlier feedback
   880→                                        </summary>
   881→                                        <div className="mt-1 space-y-1">
   882→                                          {t.feedback.slice(0, -1).map((fb, i) => (
   883→                                            <p key={i} className="rounded-lg bg-[#f5f0e5] px-2 py-1 text-xs text-[#8a7a5c]">
   884→                                              {fb.text}
   885→                                              <span className="ml-1 text-[10px] text-[#a8a08a]">{formatMessageTime(fb.at)}</span>
   886→                                            </p>
   887→                                          ))}
   888→                                        </div>
   889→                                      </details>
   890→                                    )}
   891→                                  </div>
   892→                                )}
   893→
   894→                                <div className="mt-2 flex gap-1.5">
   895→                                  {col === 'review' && (
   896→                                    <button
   897→                                      onClick={() => setPushBackTarget(t)}
   898→                                      className="btn-base btn-secondary flex-1 py-1.5 text-xs"
   899→                                    >
   900→                                      Push Back
   901→                                    </button>
   902→                                  )}
   903→                                  <button
   904→                                    onClick={() => deleteTask(t.id)}
   905→                                    className="btn-base btn-danger py-1.5 text-xs"
   906→                                  >
   907→                                    Delete
   908→                                  </button>
   909→                                </div>
   910→                              </article>
   911→                            ))}
   912→                          </div>
   913→                        </div>
   914→                      )
   915→                    })}
   916→                  </div>
   917→                </section>
   918→              </div>
   919→            )}
   920→          </main>
   921→
   922→          <aside className="flex min-h-[220px] flex-col border-t border-[var(--line-soft)] bg-white/60 xl:w-[260px] xl:shrink-0 xl:border-l xl:border-t-0">
   923→            <div className="flex items-center justify-between border-b border-[var(--line-soft)] px-4 py-3">
   924→              <div className="section-label">Live Log</div>
   925→              <button onClick={() => setLogs([])} className="text-xs font-bold text-[#688173] hover:text-[#365945]">
   926→                clear
   927→              </button>
   928→            </div>
   929→
   930→            <div ref={logScrollRef} className="min-h-0 flex-1 space-y-1 overflow-y-auto p-3 text-xs">
   931→              {logs.length === 0 && <p className="mt-6 text-center text-[#738d80]">Run an agent to stream output.</p>}
   932→
   933→              {logs.map((line) => (
   934→                <div
   935→                  key={line.id}
   936→                  className={`whitespace-pre-wrap break-all font-mono leading-relaxed ${
   937→                    line.kind === 'system' ? 'log-line-system' : line.kind === 'error' ? 'log-line-error' : 'log-line-output'
   938→                  }`}
   939→                >
   940→                  {line.kind === 'system' && <span className="text-[#819b8d]">[{line.agentId}] </span>}
   941→                  {line.text}
   942→                </div>
   943→              ))}
   944→
   945→            </div>
   946→          </aside>
   947→        </div>
   948→      </div>
   949→
   950→      {runTarget && (
   951→        <div className="modal-mask fixed inset-0 z-50 flex items-center justify-center p-4">
   952→          <div className="panel modal-card w-full max-w-xl p-5 lg:p-6">
   953→            <h3 className="font-display text-xl text-[#224232]">Run Mission</h3>
   954→            <p className="mt-1 font-mono text-xs text-[#6f877b]">{runTarget.missionId}</p>
   955→
   956→            <div className="mt-4 space-y-3">
   957→              <label className="block text-xs font-semibold text-[#668074]">
   958→                Workspace
   959→                <input
   960→                  value={runTarget.workspace}
   961→                  onChange={(e) => setRunTarget({ ...runTarget, workspace: e.target.value })}
   962→                  placeholder="/path/to/project"
   963→                  className="input-base mt-1 font-mono text-sm"
   964→                />
   965→              </label>
   966→
   967→              <label className="block text-xs font-semibold text-[#668074]">
   968→                Agent
   969→                <select

/Users/junjie/research/ClawCorp/.specify/specs/006-agent-messaging/spec.md
/Users/junjie/research/ClawCorp/.specify/specs/006-agent-messaging/plan.md

1→# Feature Specification: Agent Messaging — Direct Communication & Chat History
     2→
     3→**Feature Branch**: `006-agent-messaging`
     4→**Created**: 2026-02-22
     5→**Status**: Draft
     6→**Authors**: Chairman + Claude
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## Context
    11→
    12→### The Problem
    13→
    14→Today, ClawCorp agents can only communicate through **formal MCP tools**:
    15→
    16→| Tool | Direction | Use Case |
    17→|---|---|---|
    18→| `escalate` | Up (to supervisor) | Blocking question, waits for answer |
    19→| `report` | Up (to supervisor) | Task completion notification |
    20→| `delegate` | Down (to subordinate) | Assigns work, spawns sub-mission |
    21→| `cross_team_delegate` | Lateral (director→director) | Cross-department task handoff |
    22→
    23→This is **command-and-control**, not **communication**. Every interaction is either "I'm blocked, help" or "I'm done, here's what I did" or "Go do this."
    24→
    25→What's missing is the **everyday workplace conversation** — the kind that happens in Slack, Teams, or walking over to someone's desk:
    26→
    27→- "Hey, I noticed the auth module uses bcrypt — was there a reason you didn't pick argon2?"
    28→- "FYI: I refactored the database schema, your migration scripts might need updating"
    29→- "Quick question — what format does your API return dates in?"
    30→- "Can you review this approach before I implement it?"
    31→
    32→These aren't escalations (not blocked), reports (not done), or delegations (not assigning work). They're **peer conversations** — the glue of any functioning organization.
    33→
    34→### The Vision
    35→
    36→Replace the rigid MCP-tool-only communication with a **messaging system**:
    37→
    38→1. **Any agent can message any agent** — not just up/down the chain
    39→2. **Messages are persistent** — stored in a shared channel-like structure, browsable by Chairman
    40→3. **No blocking** — unlike `escalate` which polls for 10 minutes, messages are fire-and-forget. The recipient reads them on their next run.
    41→4. **Thread support** — reply to a specific message, creating a conversation thread
    42→5. **Chairman can see everything** — all inter-agent communication is transparent, browsable, searchable
    43→6. **Future-proof for Slack integration** — the data model mirrors Slack's channel/thread structure so we can bridge to Slack later
    44→
    45→### Analogy
    46→
    47→Think of it like an internal company Slack:
    48→
    49→```
    50→#general            → org-wide announcements
    51→#engineering        → engineering team channel
    52→#research-lab       → research team channel
    53→@senior-engineer    → direct messages to a specific agent
    54→```
    55→
    56→Agents post messages. Other agents read them on their next run. Chairman watches everything in real time from the Control Panel.
    57→
    58→---
    59→
    60→## Architecture
    61→
    62→### Channels
    63→
    64→A **channel** is a named conversation stream. Three types:
    65→
    66→| Type | Naming | Who can read/write | Example |
    67→|---|---|---|---|
    68→| `org` | `#general` | All agents + Chairman | Org-wide announcements |
    69→| `team` | `#engineering`, `#research-lab` | Team members + Chairman | Team discussions |
    70→| `direct` | `@senior-engineer:qa-engineer` | The two agents + Chairman | 1:1 conversations |
    71→
    72→Channels are created automatically:
    73→- `#general` exists by default
    74→- `#engineering`, `#research-lab`, `#product` created when teams exist
    75→- Direct channels created on first message between two agents
    76→
    77→### Messages
    78→
    79→```json
    80→{
    81→  "id": "CM-1740200000000-1",
    82→  "channel": "#engineering",
    83→  "from": "senior-engineer",
    84→  "text": "Heads up: I refactored the auth module to use JWT. If your code imports from auth/session.ts, it's now auth/jwt.ts",
    85→  "replyTo": null,
    86→  "mentions": ["qa-engineer", "intern"],
    87→  "ts": "2026-02-22T10:15:00Z"
    88→}
    89→```
    90→
    91→Replies form threads:
    92→```json
    93→{
    94→  "id": "CM-1740200000001-2",
    95→  "channel": "#engineering",
    96→  "from": "qa-engineer",
    97→  "text": "Got it — I'll update my test imports. Do the function signatures stay the same?",
    98→  "replyTo": "CM-1740200000000-1",
    99→  "mentions": ["senior-engineer"],
   100→  "ts": "2026-02-22T10:15:30Z"
   101→}
   102→```
   103→
   104→### Storage
   105→
   106→```
   107→channels/
   108→├── _channels.json          ← channel registry
   109→├── general/
   110→│   └── messages.jsonl      ← append-only log (one JSON per line)
   111→├── engineering/
   112→│   └── messages.jsonl
   113→├── research-lab/
   114→│   └── messages.jsonl
   115→└── dm-senior-engineer-qa-engineer/
   116→    └── messages.jsonl
   117→```
   118→
   119→Using **JSONL** (one JSON object per line) for channels because:
   120→- Append-only (no rewrite on every message)
   121→- Efficiently tail-able for recent messages
   122→- Each line is a self-contained message
   123→
   124→### Agent Interface — `send_message` MCP Tool
   125→
   126→Agents get a new, simple MCP tool:
   127→
   128→```
   129→send_message(
   130→  to: string,        // channel "#engineering" or agent "@qa-engineer"
   131→  text: string,       // message content (markdown ok)
   132→  reply_to?: string   // optional message ID to thread on
   133→) → { messageId: string }
   134→```
   135→
   136→This replaces **nothing** — escalate/report/delegate stay as they are for their specific workflows. `send_message` is for everything else.
   137→
   138→### Agent Interface — `read_messages` MCP Tool
   139→
   140→```
   141→read_messages(
   142→  channel?: string,    // "#engineering" or "@architect" — omit for all unread
   143→  limit?: number       // default 20
   144→) → { messages: Message[] }
   145→```
   146→
   147→Returns recent messages in a channel, or all unread messages across channels mentioning this agent.
   148→
   149→### How Agents Discover Messages
   150→
   151→On each agent run, the system prompt is augmented with:
   152→
   153→```
   154→You have {N} unread messages. Use read_messages() to check them before starting work.
   155→```
   156→
   157→This is a **hint, not a mandate**. Agents can ignore it if their task is urgent. But it encourages agents to stay aware of organizational context.
   158→
   159→---
   160→
   161→## User Scenarios & Testing
   162→
   163→### User Story 1 — Agent sends a message to a peer (Priority: P1)
   164→
   165→As an agent, I want to send a message to another agent — not as an escalation or delegation, but as a simple FYI, question, or coordination note.
   166→
   167→**Independent Test**: senior-engineer calls `send_message(to: "@qa-engineer", text: "I'm about to refactor the test helpers, hold off on writing new tests")`. Message appears in the direct channel and in the Control Panel.
   168→
   169→**Acceptance Scenarios**:
   170→
   171→1. **Given** senior-engineer sends a message to @qa-engineer, **When** qa-engineer runs next, **Then** qa-engineer can call `read_messages()` and see the message
   172→2. **Given** a message is sent, **When** Chairman views the inbox, **Then** the message appears in the chat log with sender, recipient, timestamp
   173→3. **Given** a message has `mentions: ["intern"]`, **When** intern runs, **Then** intern sees the message in their unread list
   174→
   175→---
   176→
   177→### User Story 2 — Team channel discussions (Priority: P1)
   178→
   179→As an agent, I want to post a message to my team's channel so all teammates are informed.
   180→
   181→**Independent Test**: architect posts to `#engineering`: "Architecture review: I'm moving to a monorepo structure. See /docs/adr-005.md". All engineering team members can read it.
   182→
   183→**Acceptance Scenarios**:
   184→
   185→1. **Given** architect posts to #engineering, **When** any engineering agent calls `read_messages(channel: "#engineering")`, **Then** they see the message
   186→2. **Given** a message is posted to #engineering, **When** Chairman views the channel, **Then** the message is visible with full context
   187→3. **Given** research-assistant tries to post to #engineering, **When** they're not in the Engineering team, **Then** they still can post (channels are open by default — trust the agents)
   188→
   189→---
   190→
   191→### User Story 3 — Chairman browses all conversations (Priority: P1)
   192→
   193→As Chairman, I want to browse all inter-agent communication — see what agents are discussing, who's talking to whom, what decisions are being made informally.
   194→
   195→**Independent Test**: Open a "Chat" tab in Control Panel. See a list of channels. Click a channel to see its messages as a chat timeline. Click a thread to see the replies.
   196→
   197→**Acceptance Scenarios**:
   198→
   199→1. **Given** 3 channels have messages, **When** I open the Chat tab, **Then** I see all channels listed with message counts and last activity time
   200→2. **Given** I click #engineering, **When** the channel opens, **Then** I see messages in chronological order, each showing: sender avatar, name, timestamp, text
   201→3. **Given** a message has replies (thread), **When** I click "N replies", **Then** the thread expands inline showing all replies
   202→
   203→---
   204→
   205→### User Story 4 — Message threads for focused discussions (Priority: P2)
   206→
   207→As an agent, I want to reply to a specific message (creating a thread) to keep discussions organized.
   208→
   209→**Acceptance Scenarios**:
   210→
   211→1. **Given** qa-engineer reads a message from senior-engineer, **When** qa-engineer calls `send_message(to: "#engineering", text: "...", reply_to: "CM-xxx")`, **Then** the reply is linked to the original message as a thread
   212→2. **Given** a message has 3 replies, **When** Chairman views the channel, **Then** the original message shows "3 replies" and the thread is collapsible
   213→
   214→---
   215→
   216→### User Story 5 — Chairman sends messages to agents (Priority: P2)
   217→
   218→As Chairman, I want to send a message to an agent or a channel — to give context, instructions, or feedback outside of formal missions.
   219→
   220→**Acceptance Scenarios**:
   221→
   222→1. **Given** I type a message in #general from the UI, **When** I send it, **Then** it appears in the channel as from "chairman"
   223→2. **Given** I DM @senior-engineer, **When** senior-engineer runs next, **Then** the message appears in their unread
   224→
   225→---
   226→
   227→### User Story 6 — Slack bridge (Priority: P3, future)
   228→
   229→As Chairman, I want agent messages to be mirrored to a real Slack workspace — so I can read agent conversations on my phone and reply from Slack.
   230→
   231→**Acceptance Scenarios**:
   232→
   233→1. **Given** Slack integration is configured, **When** an agent posts to #engineering, **Then** the message appears in the linked Slack channel
   234→2. **Given** I reply in Slack, **When** the agent runs next, **Then** it sees my Slack reply as a regular message
   235→
   236→---
   237→
   238→### Edge Cases
   239→
   240→- What if an agent sends a message during a mission and the mission fails? → Message is still stored. Messages are independent of mission lifecycle.
   241→- What if two agents message each other simultaneously? → No conflict — JSONL is append-only, timestamps resolve ordering.
   242→- Should agents be required to read messages before working? → No. The prompt hint is advisory. Some tasks are urgent and shouldn't block on catching up.
   243→- Can agents send messages when not running a mission? → No. Agents only exist when spawned by AgentRunner. Messages are sent during active runs.
   244→- Message size limit? → 4000 characters per message (roughly 1 Slack message). Longer content should be written to a file and linked.
   245→- Rate limiting? → No explicit limit. If an agent spams, it wastes its own tokens. The Chairman can see it in logs.
   246→- Do channels need to be created explicitly? → Team channels auto-created from teams. DM channels auto-created on first message. `#general` always exists.
   247→
   248→---
   249→
   250→## Requirements
   251→
   252→### Data Model
   253→
   254→**Channel registry** (`channels/_channels.json`):
   255→```json
   256→{
   257→  "channels": [
   258→    { "id": "general", "name": "#general", "type": "org", "created_at": "..." },
   259→    { "id": "engineering", "name": "#engineering", "type": "team", "teamId": "engineering", "created_at": "..." },
   260→    { "id": "dm-senior-engineer-qa-engineer", "name": "@senior-engineer ↔ @qa-engineer", "type": "direct", "participants": ["senior-engineer", "qa-engineer"], "created_at": "..." }
   261→  ]
   262→}
   263→```
   264→
   265→**Message** (one line in `channels/{channelId}/messages.jsonl`):
   266→```json
   267→{"id":"CM-1740200000000-1","channel":"engineering","from":"architect","text":"...","replyTo":null,"mentions":[],"ts":"2026-02-22T10:15:00Z"}
   268→```
   269→
   270→### Server Changes
   271→
   272→**`server/src/routes/channels.ts`** (new) — Channel & message API:
   273→- `GET /api/channels` → list all channels with metadata (message count, last activity)
   274→- `GET /api/channels/:id/messages?limit=50&before=` → paginated messages for a channel
   275→- `POST /api/channels/:id/messages` → post a message (from Chairman UI)
   276→- `POST /api/channels/send` → agent sends a message (called by MCP tool)
   277→- `GET /api/channels/unread/:agentId` → get unread messages mentioning an agent
   278→
   279→**`server/src/mcp/server.ts`** (edit) — add agent tools:
   280→- `send_message` tool — posts to a channel or DM
   281→- `read_messages` tool — reads recent or unread messages
   282→
   283→**`server/src/lib/AgentRunner.ts`** (edit) — unread message hint:
   284→- Before spawning agent, count unread messages for this agent
   285→- If > 0, append to system prompt: `"You have {N} unread messages. Use read_messages() to check them."`
   286→
   287→### Client Changes
   288→
   289→**`client/src/App.tsx`** (edit) — add Chat tab:
   290→- New `chat` tab alongside tasks, inbox, agents
   291→- Left sidebar: channel list with unread indicators
   292→- Main area: message timeline for selected channel
   293→- Thread expansion (click "N replies" to expand)
   294→- Message input at bottom for Chairman to send messages
   295→- WebSocket handlers for `chat:message` events
   296→
   297→### WebSocket Events
   298→
   299→- `chat:message` → `{ channel, message }` — real-time message delivery
   300→- `chat:channel_created` → `{ channel }` — new channel appeared
   301→
   302→### New Files
   303→
   304→| File | Purpose |
   305→|---|---|
   306→| `server/src/routes/channels.ts` | Channel & message REST API |
   307→| `channels/_channels.json` | Channel registry (auto-created) |
   308→| `channels/{id}/messages.jsonl` | Append-only message log per channel |
   309→
   310→### .gitignore
   311→
   312→Add `channels/` to .gitignore (runtime data).
   313→
   314→---
   315→
   316→## Implementation Strategy
   317→
   318→### Phase 1 — Server infrastructure (P1)
   319→1. Create channel data model and storage (JSONL)
   320→2. Implement channels route with CRUD + message posting
   321→3. Auto-create team channels from existing teams
   322→4. Auto-create `#general` on first access
   323→

# Feature Specification: Agent Messaging — Direct Communication & Chat History

**Feature Branch**: `006-agent-messaging`
**Created**: 2026-02-22
**Status**: Draft
**Authors**: Chairman + Claude

---

## Context

### The Problem

Today, ClawCorp agents can only communicate through **formal MCP tools**:

| Tool | Direction | Use Case |
|---|---|---|
| `escalate` | Up (to supervisor) | Blocking question, waits for answer |
| `report` | Up (to supervisor) | Task completion notification |
| `delegate` | Down (to subordinate) | Assigns work, spawns sub-mission |
| `cross_team_delegate` | Lateral (director→director) | Cross-department task handoff |

This is **command-and-control**, not **communication**. Every interaction is either "I'm blocked, help" or "I'm done, here's what I did" or "Go do this."

What's missing is the **everyday workplace conversation** — the kind that happens in Slack, Teams, or walking over to someone's desk:

- "Hey, I noticed the auth module uses bcrypt — was there a reason you didn't pick argon2?"
- "FYI: I refactored the database schema, your migration scripts might need updating"
- "Quick question — what format does your API return dates in?"
- "Can you review this approach before I implement it?"

These aren't escalations (not blocked), reports (not done), or delegations (not assigning work). They're **peer conversations** — the glue of any functioning organization.

### The Vision

Replace the rigid MCP-tool-only communication with a **messaging system**:

1. **Any agent can message any agent** — not just up/down the chain
2. **Messages are persistent** — stored in a shared channel-like structure, browsable by Chairman
3. **No blocking** — unlike `escalate` which polls for 10 minutes, messages are fire-and-forget. The recipient reads them on their next run.
4. **Thread support** — reply to a specific message, creating a conversation thread
5. **Chairman can see everything** — all inter-agent communication is transparent, browsable, searchable
6. **Future-proof for Slack integration** — the data model mirrors Slack's channel/thread structure so we can bridge to Slack later

### Analogy

Think of it like an internal company Slack:

```
#general            → org-wide announcements
#engineering        → engineering team channel
#research-lab       → research team channel
@senior-engineer    → direct messages to a specific agent
```

Agents post messages. Other agents read them on their next run. Chairman watches everything in real time from the Control Panel.

---

## Architecture

### Channels

A **channel** is a named conversation stream. Three types:

| Type | Naming | Who can read/write | Example |
|---|---|---|---|
| `org` | `#general` | All agents + Chairman | Org-wide announcements |
| `team` | `#engineering`, `#research-lab` | Team members + Chairman | Team discussions |
| `direct` | `@senior-engineer:qa-engineer` | The two agents + Chairman | 1:1 conversations |

Channels are created automatically:
- `#general` exists by default
- `#engineering`, `#research-lab`, `#product` created when teams exist
- Direct channels created on first message between two agents

### Messages

```json
{
  "id": "CM-1740200000000-1",
  "channel": "#engineering",
  "from": "senior-engineer",
  "text": "Heads up: I refactored the auth module to use JWT. If your code imports from auth/session.ts, it's now auth/jwt.ts",
  "replyTo": null,
  "mentions": ["qa-engineer", "intern"],
  "ts": "2026-02-22T10:15:00Z"
}
```

Replies form threads:
```json
{
  "id": "CM-1740200000001-2",
  "channel": "#engineering",
  "from": "qa-engineer",
  "text": "Got it — I'll update my test imports. Do the function signatures stay the same?",
  "replyTo": "CM-1740200000000-1",
  "mentions": ["senior-engineer"],
  "ts": "2026-02-22T10:15:30Z"
}
```

### Storage

```
channels/
├── _channels.json          ← channel registry
├── general/
│   └── messages.jsonl      ← append-only log (one JSON per line)
├── engineering/
│   └── messages.jsonl
├── research-lab/
│   └── messages.jsonl
└── dm-senior-engineer-qa-engineer/
    └── messages.jsonl
```

Using **JSONL** (one JSON object per line) for channels because:
- Append-only (no rewrite on every message)
- Efficiently tail-able for recent messages
- Each line is a self-contained message

### Agent Interface — `send_message` MCP Tool

Agents get a new, simple MCP tool:

```
send_message(
  to: string,        // channel "#engineering" or agent "@qa-engineer"
  text: string,       // message content (markdown ok)
  reply_to?: string   // optional message ID to thread on
) → { messageId: string }
```

This replaces **nothing** — escalate/report/delegate stay as they are for their specific workflows. `send_message` is for everything else.

### Agent Interface — `read_messages` MCP Tool

```
read_messages(
  channel?: string,    // "#engineering" or "@architect" — omit for all unread
  limit?: number       // default 20
) → { messages: Message[] }
```

Returns recent messages in a channel, or all unread messages across channels mentioning this agent.

### How Agents Discover Messages

On each agent run, the system prompt is augmented with:

```
You have {N} unread messages. Use read_messages() to check them before starting work.
```

This is a **hint, not a mandate**. Agents can ignore it if their task is urgent. But it encourages agents to stay aware of organizational context.

---

## User Scenarios & Testing

### User Story 1 — Agent sends a message to a peer (Priority: P1)

As an agent, I want to send a message to another agent — not as an escalation or delegation, but as a simple FYI, question, or coordination note.

**Independent Test**: senior-engineer calls `send_message(to: "@qa-engineer", text: "I'm about to refactor the test helpers, hold off on writing new tests")`. Message appears in the direct channel and in the Control Panel.

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** senior-engineer sends a message to @qa-engineer, **When** qa-engineer runs next, **Then** qa-engineer can call `read_messages()` and see the message
2. **Given** a message is sent, **When** Chairman views the inbox, **Then** the message appears in the chat log with sender, recipient, timestamp
3. **Given** a message has `mentions: ["intern"]`, **When** intern runs, **Then** intern sees the message in their unread list

---

### User Story 2 — Team channel discussions (Priority: P1)

As an agent, I want to post a message to my team's channel so all teammates are informed.

**Independent Test**: architect posts to `#engineering`: "Architecture review: I'm moving to a monorepo structure. See /docs/adr-005.md". All engineering team members can read it.

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** architect posts to #engineering, **When** any engineering agent calls `read_messages(channel: "#engineering")`, **Then** they see the message
2. **Given** a message is posted to #engineering, **When** Chairman views the channel, **Then** the message is visible with full context
3. **Given** research-assistant tries to post to #engineering, **When** they're not in the Engineering team, **Then** they still can post (channels are open by default — trust the agents)

---

### User Story 3 — Chairman browses all conversations (Priority: P1)

As Chairman, I want to browse all inter-agent communication — see what agents are discussing, who's talking to whom, what decisions are being made informally.

**Independent Test**: Open a "Chat" tab in Control Panel. See a list of channels. Click a channel to see its messages as a chat timeline. Click a thread to see the replies.

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** 3 channels have messages, **When** I open the Chat tab, **Then** I see all channels listed with message counts and last activity time
2. **Given** I click #engineering, **When** the channel opens, **Then** I see messages in chronological order, each showing: sender avatar, name, timestamp, text
3. **Given** a message has replies (thread), **When** I click "N replies", **Then** the thread expands inline showing all replies

---

### User Story 4 — Message threads for focused discussions (Priority: P2)

As an agent, I want to reply to a specific message (creating a thread) to keep discussions organized.

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** qa-engineer reads a message from senior-engineer, **When** qa-engineer calls `send_message(to: "#engineering", text: "...", reply_to: "CM-xxx")`, **Then** the reply is linked to the original message as a thread
2. **Given** a message has 3 replies, **When** Chairman views the channel, **Then** the original message shows "3 replies" and the thread is collapsible

---

### User Story 5 — Chairman sends messages to agents (Priority: P2)

As Chairman, I want to send a message to an agent or a channel — to give context, instructions, or feedback outside of formal missions.

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** I type a message in #general from the UI, **When** I send it, **Then** it appears in the channel as from "chairman"
2. **Given** I DM @senior-engineer, **When** senior-engineer runs next, **Then** the message appears in their unread

---

### User Story 6 — Slack bridge (Priority: P3, future)

As Chairman, I want agent messages to be mirrored to a real Slack workspace — so I can read agent conversations on my phone and reply from Slack.

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** Slack integration is configured, **When** an agent posts to #engineering, **Then** the message appears in the linked Slack channel
2. **Given** I reply in Slack, **When** the agent runs next, **Then** it sees my Slack reply as a regular message

---

### Edge Cases

- What if an agent sends a message during a mission and the mission fails? → Message is still stored. Messages are independent of mission lifecycle.
- What if two agents message each other simultaneously? → No conflict — JSONL is append-only, timestamps resolve ordering.
- Should agents be required to read messages before working? → No. The prompt hint is advisory. Some tasks are urgent and shouldn't block on catching up.
- Can agents send messages when not running a mission? → No. Agents only exist when spawned by AgentRunner. Messages are sent during active runs.
- Message size limit? → 4000 characters per message (roughly 1 Slack message). Longer content should be written to a file and linked.
- Rate limiting? → No explicit limit. If an agent spams, it wastes its own tokens. The Chairman can see it in logs.
- Do channels need to be created explicitly? → Team channels auto-created from teams. DM channels auto-created on first message. `#general` always exists.

---

## Requirements

### Data Model

**Channel registry** (`channels/_channels.json`):
```json
{
  "channels": [
    { "id": "general", "name": "#general", "type": "org", "created_at": "..." },
    { "id": "engineering", "name": "#engineering", "type": "team", "teamId": "engineering", "created_at": "..." },
    { "id": "dm-senior-engineer-qa-engineer", "name": "@senior-engineer ↔ @qa-engineer", "type": "direct", "participants": ["senior-engineer", "qa-engineer"], "created_at": "..." }
  ]
}
```

**Message** (one line in `channels/{channelId}/messages.jsonl`):
```json
{"id":"CM-1740200000000-1","channel":"engineering","from":"architect","text":"...","replyTo":null,"mentions":[],"ts":"2026-02-22T10:15:00Z"}
```

### Server Changes

**`server/src/routes/channels.ts`** (new) — Channel & message API:
- `GET /api/channels` → list all channels with metadata (message count, last activity)
- `GET /api/channels/:id/messages?limit=50&before=` → paginated messages for a channel
- `POST /api/channels/:id/messages` → post a message (from Chairman UI)
- `POST /api/channels/send` → agent sends a message (called by MCP tool)
- `GET /api/channels/unread/:agentId` → get unread messages mentioning an agent

**`server/src/mcp/server.ts`** (edit) — add agent tools:
- `send_message` tool — posts to a channel or DM
- `read_messages` tool — reads recent or unread messages

**`server/src/lib/AgentRunner.ts`** (edit) — unread message hint:
- Before spawning agent, count unread messages for this agent
- If > 0, append to system prompt: `"You have {N} unread messages. Use read_messages() to check them."`

### Client Changes

**`client/src/App.tsx`** (edit) — add Chat tab:
- New `chat` tab alongside tasks, inbox, agents
- Left sidebar: channel list with unread indicators
- Main area: message timeline for selected channel
- Thread expansion (click "N replies" to expand)
- Message input at bottom for Chairman to send messages
- WebSocket handlers for `chat:message` events

### WebSocket Events

- `chat:message` → `{ channel, message }` — real-time message delivery
- `chat:channel_created` → `{ channel }` — new channel appeared

### New Files

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `server/src/routes/channels.ts` | Channel & message REST API |
| `channels/_channels.json` | Channel registry (auto-created) |
| `channels/{id}/messages.jsonl` | Append-only message log per channel |

### .gitignore

Add `channels/` to .gitignore (runtime data).

---

## Implementation Strategy

### Phase 1 — Server infrastructure (P1)
1. Create channel data model and storage (JSONL)
2. Implement channels route with CRUD + message posting
3. Auto-create team channels from existing teams
4. Auto-create `#general` on first access

### Phase 2 — Agent MCP tools (P1)
1. Add `send_message` and `read_messages` MCP tools
2. Add unread message hint to AgentRunner prompt injection
3. Test with manual agent runs

### Phase 3 — Client Chat tab (P1)
1. Add Chat tab with channel list sidebar
2. Message timeline view with sender, timestamp, text
3. Thread collapse/expand
4. Chairman message input
5. Real-time updates via WebSocket

### Phase 4 — Slack bridge (P3, future)
1. Slack app integration (OAuth)
2. Channel mapping: ClawCorp channel ↔ Slack channel
3. Bidirectional message sync
4. Slack reply → ClawCorp message

---

## Relationship to Existing Systems

### What stays the same

| System | Role | Still Used For |
|---|---|---|
| `escalate` MCP tool | Blocking question to supervisor | Agent is stuck, needs answer before continuing |
| `report` MCP tool | Task completion report | Agent finishes mission, sends summary |
| `delegate` MCP tool | Assign work to subordinate | Manager spawns sub-mission for subordinate |
| Inbox tab | Chairman answers escalations | Viewing and responding to escalations |

### What `send_message` adds

| Use Case | Before (workaround) | After |
|---|---|---|
| FYI to teammate | No mechanism (lost context) | `send_message(to: "@qa-engineer", text: "...")` |
| Team announcement | No mechanism | `send_message(to: "#engineering", text: "...")` |
| Ask peer a question | `escalate` (wrong semantics, blocks agent) | `send_message` (non-blocking, no hierarchy required) |
| Coordinate on shared work | Rely on mission descriptions | Real-time channel discussion |
| Chairman gives informal feedback | Create a new mission (overkill) | DM or channel message |

### Future: Slack Bridge Architecture (P3)

```
Agent calls send_message("#engineering", "Refactored auth module")
    ↓
ClawCorp channels route saves message
    ↓
If Slack bridge enabled:
    ↓
POST to Slack API → message appears in #clawcorp-engineering
    ↓
Someone replies in Slack
    ↓
Slack Event API webhook → ClawCorp channels route
    ↓
Message saved with from: "slack:username"
    ↓
Agent reads it on next run via read_messages()
```

---

## Success Criteria

- **SC-001**: Agents can send and read messages to/from any agent or channel
- **SC-002**: Chairman can browse all channels and read all conversations
- **SC-003**: Messages persist across server restarts and agent runs
- **SC-004**: Unread message count is shown to agents on their next run
- **SC-005**: Thread replies are visually grouped in the UI
- **SC-006**: Chat tab loads within 1 second with 500+ messages across channels
- **SC-007**: Data model is compatible with future Slack bridge integration

# Plan: Implement Spec 006 — Agent Messaging

## Summary

Add a persistent messaging system where agents can send messages to peers, teams, or the whole org. Chairman sees all conversations in a new Chat tab. Future-proof for Slack bridge.

## Implementation (4 commits)

### Commit 1: Server — Channels route + message storage

**File: `server/src/routes/channels.ts`** (new)

Channel and message API with JSONL storage:

- `GET /api/channels` — list all channels with metadata (last message, message count)
- `GET /api/channels/:id/messages?limit=50&before=` — paginated messages
- `POST /api/channels/:id/messages` — post message (Chairman UI)
- `POST /api/channels/send` — agent sends message (MCP tool calls this)
- `GET /api/channels/unread/:agentId` — unread messages mentioning agent

Storage: `channels/{channelId}/messages.jsonl` (append-only, one JSON per line).

Auto-create channels:
- `#general` on first access
- Team channels from existing teams on server start
- DM channels on first direct message

Helper functions:
- `ensureChannel(id, name, type)` — create channel dir + registry entry if not exists
- `appendMessage(channelId, msg)` — append to JSONL
- `readMessages(channelId, limit, before)` — tail JSONL for recent messages
- `getUnread(agentId)` — scan channels for messages mentioning agent since last read

**File: `server/src/index.ts`** (edit)
- Mount `channelRoutes` at `/api/channels`

**File: `.gitignore`** (edit)
- Add `channels/`

### Commit 2: MCP — send_message + read_messages tools

**File: `server/src/mcp/server.ts`** (edit)

Add two new MCP tools:

1. `send_message(to, text, reply_to?)`:
   - `to` is either `"#channel-name"` or `"@agent-id"`
   - For `@agent-id`, auto-creates DM channel `dm-{sorted-pair}`
   - Calls `POST /api/channels/send` internally
   - Returns `{ messageId }`

2. `read_messages(channel?, limit?)`:
   - If channel specified, reads that channel
   - If omitted, returns unread messages across all channels mentioning this agent
   - Calls `GET /api/channels/:id/messages` or `GET /api/channels/unread/:agentId`
   - Returns formatted message list

**File: `server/src/lib/AgentRunner.ts`** (edit)

Before spawning agent, check unread count:
- Call internal `getUnreadCount(agentId)`
- If > 0, append to full prompt: `"\n\nYou have {N} unread messages. Use read_messages() to check them before starting work."`

### Commit 3: Client — Chat tab

**File: `client/src/App.tsx`** (edit)

Add `chat` as a fourth tab:

- **Channel sidebar** (left): list of channels with names, unread dots, last activity
- **Message timeline** (main area): messages for selected channel, chronological, chat-bubble style
- **Thread support**: messages with replies show "N replies" — click to expand inline
- **Message input**: textarea at bottom for Chairman to send messages to the selected channel
- **WebSocket**: handle `chat:message` and `chat:channel_created` events

State additions:
- `channels: Channel[]` — fetched from `/api/channels`
- `selectedChannel: string | null` — currently viewing
- `channelMessages: Message[]` — messages for selected channel
- `chatInput: string` — Chairman's message draft

New types:
```typescript
type Channel = {
  id: string
  name: string
  type: 'org' | 'team' | 'direct'
  lastActivity?: string
  messageCount?: number
}

type ChatMessage = {
  id: string
  channel: string
  from: string
  text: string
  replyTo: string | null
  mentions: string[]
  ts: string
}
```

### Commit 4: Auto-create team channels + polish

- On server start, scan existing teams and create `#engineering`, `#research-lab`, `#product` channels
- When a new team is created (`POST /api/teams`), auto-create its channel
- Add team channel creation to teams route

## Files to modify

| File | Action |
|---|---|
| `server/src/routes/channels.ts` | **Create** — channels + messages API |
| `server/src/index.ts` | **Edit** — mount channel routes |
| `server/src/mcp/server.ts` | **Edit** — add send_message + read_messages tools |
| `server/src/lib/AgentRunner.ts` | **Edit** — unread message prompt hint |
| `server/src/routes/teams.ts` | **Edit** — auto-create team channel on team creation |
| `client/src/App.tsx` | **Edit** — add Chat tab |
| `.gitignore` | **Edit** — add channels/ |

## Key decisions

- **JSONL storage** — append-only, no full-file rewrite on each message
- **Non-blocking** — `send_message` returns immediately, no polling
- **Open channels** — any agent can post to any channel (trust model)
- **DM auto-creation** — direct channels created on first message, sorted pair for consistency
- **Unread tracking** — simple approach: store last-read timestamp per agent per channel
- **No message editing/deletion** — messages are immutable (append-only log)

content.content:
1→import { Hono } from 'hono'
     2→import { readdir, readFile, writeFile, mkdir, rm } from 'fs/promises'
     3→import { join } from 'path'
     4→import { existsSync } from 'fs'
     5→import { broadcast } from '../lib/hub'
     6→
     7→export const teamRoutes = new Hono()
     8→
     9→const TEAMS_DIR = join(import.meta.dir, '../../../teams')
    10→const AGENTS_DIR = join(import.meta.dir, '../../../agents')
    11→
    12→async function readTeam(id: string) {
    13→  const teamPath = join(TEAMS_DIR, id, 'team.json')
    14→  return JSON.parse(await readFile(teamPath, 'utf-8'))
    15→}
    16→
    17→async function writeTeam(id: string, team: Record<string, unknown>) {
    18→  const dir = join(TEAMS_DIR, id)
    19→  await mkdir(dir, { recursive: true })
    20→  await writeFile(join(dir, 'team.json'), JSON.stringify(team, null, 2))
    21→}
    22→
    23→// GET /api/teams — list all teams with resolved members
    24→teamRoutes.get('/', async (c) => {
    25→  await mkdir(TEAMS_DIR, { recursive: true })
    26→  const dirs = await readdir(TEAMS_DIR, { withFileTypes: true })
    27→  const teams = await Promise.all(
    28→    dirs
    29→      .filter((d) => d.isDirectory())
    30→      .map(async (d) => {
    31→        try {
    32→          return await readTeam(d.name)
    33→        } catch {
    34→          return null
    35→        }
    36→      })
    37→  )
    38→  return c.json(teams.filter(Boolean))
    39→})
    40→
    41→// GET /api/teams/:id — single team
    42→teamRoutes.get('/:id', async (c) => {
    43→  const id = c.req.param('id')
    44→  try {
    45→    const team = await readTeam(id)
    46→    return c.json(team)
    47→  } catch {
    48→    return c.json({ error: 'Team not found' }, 404)
    49→  }
    50→})
    51→
    52→// POST /api/teams — create a new team
    53→teamRoutes.post('/', async (c) => {
    54→  const body = await c.req.json()
    55→  const { id, name, executive_sponsor, head } = body
    56→
    57→  if (!id || !name) return c.json({ error: 'id and name required' }, 400)
    58→
    59→  const teamDir = join(TEAMS_DIR, id)
    60→  if (existsSync(teamDir)) return c.json({ error: `Team ${id} already exists` }, 409)
    61→
    62→  const team = {
    63→    id,
    64→    name,
    65→    executive_sponsor: executive_sponsor ?? '',
    66→    head: head ?? '',
    67→    members: body.members ?? [],
    68→    created_at: new Date().toISOString(),
    69→  }
    70→
    71→  await writeTeam(id, team)
    72→  broadcast('team:created', team)
    73→  return c.json(team, 201)
    74→})
    75→
    76→// PATCH /api/teams/:id — update team
    77→teamRoutes.patch('/:id', async (c) => {
    78→  const id = c.req.param('id')
    79→  try {
    80→    const team = await readTeam(id)
    81→    const body = await c.req.json()
    82→
    83→    if (body.name !== undefined) team.name = body.name
    84→    if (body.executive_sponsor !== undefined) team.executive_sponsor = body.executive_sponsor
    85→    if (body.head !== undefined) team.head = body.head
    86→    if (body.members !== undefined) team.members = body.members
    87→
    88→    // Add member
    89→    if (body.add_member) {
    90→      if (!team.members.includes(body.add_member)) {
    91→        team.members.push(body.add_member)
    92→        // Update agent profile
    93→        try {
    94→          const profilePath = join(AGENTS_DIR, body.add_member, 'profile.json')
    95→          const profile = JSON.parse(await readFile(profilePath, 'utf-8'))
    96→          profile.team = id
    97→          profile.reports_to = team.head
    98→          await writeFile(profilePath, JSON.stringify(profile, null, 2))
    99→        } catch {}
   100→      }
   101→    }
   102→
   103→    // Remove member
   104→    if (body.remove_member) {
   105→      team.members = team.members.filter((m: string) => m !== body.remove_member)
   106→      try {
   107→        const profilePath = join(AGENTS_DIR, body.remove_member, 'profile.json')
   108→        const profile = JSON.parse(await readFile(profilePath, 'utf-8'))
   109→        delete profile.team
   110→        profile.reports_to = 'chairman'
   111→        await writeFile(profilePath, JSON.stringify(profile, null, 2))
   112→      } catch {}
   113→    }
   114→
   115→    await writeTeam(id, team)
   116→    broadcast('team:updated', team)
   117→    return c.json(team)
   118→  } catch {
   119→    return c.json({ error: 'Team not found' }, 404)
   120→  }
   121→})
   122→
   123→// DELETE /api/teams/:id — delete team, unassign members
   124→teamRoutes.delete('/:id', async (c) => {
   125→  const id = c.req.param('id')
   126→  const teamDir = join(TEAMS_DIR, id)
   127→
   128→  if (!existsSync(teamDir)) return c.json({ error: 'Team not found' }, 404)
   129→
   130→  // Unassign members
   131→  try {
   132→    const team = await readTeam(id)
   133→    const allMembers = [team.head, ...team.members].filter(Boolean)
   134→    for (const memberId of allMembers) {
   135→      try {
   136→        const profilePath = join(AGENTS_DIR, memberId, 'profile.json')
   137→        const profile = JSON.parse(await readFile(profilePath, 'utf-8'))
   138→        delete profile.team
   139→        await writeFile(profilePath, JSON.stringify(profile, null, 2))
   140→      } catch {}
   141→    }
   142→  } catch {}
   143→
   144→  await rm(teamDir, { recursive: true })
   145→  broadcast('team:deleted', { id })
   146→  return c.json({ ok: true })
   147→})
   148→

import { Hono } from 'hono'
import { cors } from 'hono/cors'
import { logger } from 'hono/logger'
import { addClient, removeClient } from './lib/hub'
import { agentRoutes } from './routes/agents'
import { missionRoutes } from './routes/missions'
import { runRoutes } from './routes/run'
import { messageRoutes } from './routes/messages'
import { delegateRoutes } from './routes/delegate'
import { hireRoutes } from './routes/hire'
import { taskRoutes } from './routes/tasks'
import { teamRoutes } from './routes/teams'
import { channelRoutes } from './routes/channels'
import { initChannels } from './routes/channels'

const app = new Hono()

app.use('*', cors({ origin: 'http://localhost:5173' }))
app.use('*', logger())

app.route('/api/agents', agentRoutes)
app.route('/api/missions', missionRoutes)
app.route('/api/run', runRoutes)
app.route('/api/messages', messageRoutes)
app.route('/api/delegate', delegateRoutes)
app.route('/api/hire', hireRoutes)
app.route('/api/tasks', taskRoutes)
app.route('/api/teams', teamRoutes)
app.route('/api/channels', channelRoutes)

app.get('/health', (c) => c.json({ status: 'ok', time: new Date().toISOString() }))

const server = Bun.serve({
  port: 3001,
  fetch(req, server) {
    if (req.headers.get('upgrade') === 'websocket') {
      const ok = server.upgrade(req)
      if (ok) return undefined
      return new Response('WebSocket upgrade failed', { status: 400 })
    }
    return app.fetch(req)
  },
  websocket: {
    open(ws) { addClient(ws) },
    close(ws) { removeClient(ws) },
    message() {}
  }
})

console.log(`ClawCorp server → http://localhost:${server.port}`)

content.text:
All done. Here's a summary of what was implemented across 3 commits:

### Commit 1: `feat(server): add channels route with JSONL message storage`
- **`server/src/routes/channels.ts`** (new) — Full channels API with JSONL append-only storage. 5 endpoints: list channels, read messages (paginated), Chairman posts, agent sends, unread for agent.
- **`server/src/index.ts`** — Mounted channel routes, `initChannels()` on startup
- **`.gitignore`** — Added `channels/`
- **`server/src/routes/teams.ts`** — Auto-creates team channel when a new team is created

### Commit 2: `feat(mcp): add send_message and read_messages tools + unread hint`
- **`server/src/mcp/server.ts`** — Two new MCP tools: `send_message(to, text, reply_to?)` and `read_messages(channel?, limit?)`
- **`server/src/lib/AgentRunner.ts`** — Checks unread count before spawning agent; if > 0, appends hint to prompt

### Commit 3: `feat(client): add Chat tab with channel sidebar and message timeline`
- **`client/src/App.tsx`** — New `chat` tab with:
  - Channel sidebar (sorted by activity, message counts)
  - Message timeline with chronological display
  - Thread support (expandable "N replies" per message)
  - Chairman message input (send as "chairman")
  - WebSocket handlers for `chat:message` and `chat:channel_created`

content.thinking:
The user is saying that the chat is for internal agent communication, and each chat should be clickable to view in real-time. They seem to be emphasizing that this is an internal chat system between agents, and they want to be able to click on each message/conversation to view it in real-time.

Let me re-read the spec more carefully... The user seems to be pointing out that the Chat tab should let the Chairman browse all inter-agent conversations in real-time - clicking on channels to see live messages as agents communicate.

Looking at my implementation, I think it already does this - you click a channel in the sidebar to view its messages, and WebSocket events update in real-time. 

Actually, I think the user might be confirming or expressing enthusiasm about this feature rather than asking for something new - the exclamation marks suggest they're describing what the system does in a positive way.

content.text:
明白！Chairman 的消息（escalation 回答、delegation）只需要传达到直接下属（C-suite 高管们），不需要穿透到更底层。现在的系统已经是这样工作的：

- `escalate` — agent 发给自己的 `reports_to`（上级）
- `delegate` — 发给自己的 subordinate
- `report` — 发给自己的 `reports_to`

Chairman 只和直属高管交互，高管再往下传递。这就是层级指挥链。

但你说的是 `send_message` 这个新的 chat 系统？你是说 agents 之间的 chat 消息也应该有**层级限制**？还是你觉得 chat 系统本身不需要，因为现有的 escalate/delegate/report 已经够用了？

content.text:
Done.消息权限现在和真实公司一样：

| 角色 | 可以发消息给 | 可以发到频道 |
|---|---|---|
| **普通 agent** | 直接上级、下属、同 team 同事 | 自己 team 的频道 + `#general` |
| **Director / Executive** | 以上 + 其他部门的 director/executive | 以上 + 任何 team 频道 |

越级或跨部门消息会被拒绝（403），并告诉 agent 原因。Chairman 的 Chat tab 只看不发。
